title: Stock Market Today: Dow Jones, S&P 500 Futures Tumble As DOJ Serves Federal Reserve—Vistra, Tempus AI, Alibaba In Focus
author: Rishabh Mishra
description: U.S. stock futures declined on Monday following tensions between the DOJ and Federal Reserve over Chair Powell's congressional testimony. Major indices fell 0.57-1.04% in premarket trading. Key movers included Vistra (up on Meta nuclear deal), Alibaba (up on AI milestone), Tempus AI (up on strong 2025 results), and XPeng (up on delivery growth). The market awaits key economic data this week including CPI and jobless claims.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock rose 0.69% in premarket after signing a 20-year nuclear power deal with Meta Platforms, indicating strong business development despite weak technical price trends.
Stock surged 4.46% after its Qwen AI series became the world's most widely used open-source AI system with over 700 million downloads, demonstrating strong market leadership in AI.
Stock jumped 10.7% after reporting record $1.1 billion in total contract value and 126% net revenue retention for 2025, showing exceptional business growth and client expansion.
Stock gained 2.75% after unveiling new flagship vehicle and autonomous driving AI while reporting 126% delivery growth in 2025, demonstrating strong international expansion.
Stock declined 0.37% despite reporting strong preliminary Q3 net sales of $705.6 million with 16% year-over-year growth, suggesting market sentiment diverged from positive earnings.
Stock fell 1.15% in premarket trading as part of broader market decline, despite positive news of nuclear power deal with Vistra.
ETF declined 0.69% in premarket as major indices fell due to DOJ-Federal Reserve tensions and broader market uncertainty.
ETF fell 0.95% in premarket, underperforming SPY as tech-heavy Nasdaq 100 index declined 1.04% amid market headwinds.

title: Boot Barn Opens at Marketplace at Seminole Towne Center
author: Cto Realty Growth, Inc.
description: CTO Realty Growth announced the grand opening of a 12,000-square-foot Boot Barn store at Marketplace at Seminole Towne Center in Orlando, Florida, enhancing the retail center's merchandising mix.

title: Bulls, Bands, and Barrels Unveils First Half of 2026 With Its Biggest Lineup Yet! Jon Pardi, Ella Langley, BigXthaPlug, Gavin Adcock, Hudson Westbrook, Sam Barber, Dylan Gossett, & More
author: Ellen Newberg
description: Bulls, Bands, and Barrels (BBB) announces its 2026 tour featuring country music artists and western sports entertainment, launching January 10th in Evansville, Indiana with performances across multiple U.S. cities.

title: Wolverine Expands Jordan Davis Partnership With Bold Lifestyle Collection
author: Triveni Kothapalli
description: Wolverine World Wide announced an expanded partnership with country singer Jordan Davis, launching a limited-edition footwear and apparel line that blends workwear heritage with modern lifestyle appeal.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company is strategically expanding brand visibility through a cultural partnership, introducing new product lines that appeal to a broader consumer base while maintaining core brand authenticity

title: Boot Barn (BOOT) Q1 Sales Rise 19%
author: Jesterai
description: Boot Barn reported Q1 FY2026 results with 19.1% revenue growth and 38.1% higher earnings per share, but missed analyst expectations. The company remains cautiously optimistic about fiscal 2026, forecasting up to 14% sales growth while anticipating potential tariff and pricing pressures.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mixed performance with strong revenue growth and operational gains, but falling short of analyst estimates and expressing caution about future margin pressures
